Name: Illustration
Description: Should relate to the content or benefit literally or metaphorically.
Content Rules:
Make complex ideas more accessible.

Should be a spot hero image that relates to the UI copy.

Can efficiently convey brand personality.

Guide people in the right direction or let them know what to expect.

Name: Title
Description: The title provides a concise overview of the contents of the journey.
Content Rules:
Use the title to communicate the main benefit in an active and personalized way, for example: "Manage your issues", instead of "Issue types."

Try to personalize where you can, for example: "A better experience for your team", instead of "Better team experiences."

Try to limit titles to three words, although four words are permissible if you are using a short article like “an”, “a”, or “the”.

Name: Description
Content Rules:
Try to keep message length to less than three lines at the product's minimum supported size.

Don't just point out what the feature or functionality is. Tell people why it's important at this point in time.

Be considerate of people's time and patience. Be short and to the point, and avoid using company jargon.

Name: CTA bar
Description: Contains the navigational and action buttons. Action buttons, such as Done, Get started, Try it, are on the right.

Name: Checkbox
Default State: By default, this check box will be unselected.

Name: Primary button
Description:
Will either have “Done” or “Try it”. Done will Close the modal dialog box.

Try it will take the user to the next flow, closing the modal.
Dynamic Behavior:
The primary CTA should NEVER be disabled. 
We should wait to validate any and all inputs when the CTA is clicked and not before.

Name: Secondary button
Description: In case the primary CTA is “Try it”, a secondary CTA “Not now” shall be present. This will close the modal and not participate in the next flow.
